title: domain.Locker port + adapter/fsstore O_EXCL impl (cross-process serialization for Phase 3+)
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 shipped single-writer fsstore (TTR-E01-S09). This story adds the per-item lock that makes concurrent writers safe — Locker.Acquire blocks until the lock is obtained or ctx is cancelled (§14.1).

fsstore impl uses os.OpenFile(path, O_RDWR|O_CREATE|O_EXCL, 0o600) on .tasks/locks/<id>.lock; release deletes the file. Stale locks (older than a configurable threshold) are reaped on acquire attempt.
Returns ErrLockTimeout per §14.3 when ctx deadlines.

fsstore.Put is updated to always acquire the per-item lock for the read-modify-write cycle once Phase 3 ships. There is no config toggle; locking is part of the correctness contract for concurrent agents.
Phase 1's Phase-1-style tests still pass with locking enabled (single-writer path is a degenerate case).

Platform scope: workspace MUST live on a local POSIX filesystem (linux/{amd64,arm64}, darwin/arm64). NFS, SMB, and other network filesystems are explicitly unsupported because O_EXCL is unreliable on them (well-known kernel races). task validate uses statfs to detect non-local filesystem types and emits an integrity warning when the workspace is hosted on one. Windows is out of scope by §12.7 (build-all does not target it).
